Ad Text:
Employment Type: Full Time (35 hours per week)
Duration: Continuing
Remuneration: Level 9 from $148k + 17% superannuation & leave loading  
Location:  Kensington NSW (2-3 days campus based each week)

Why your role matters

The Marketing & Digital Experience (M&DX) portfolio has been established to enhance UNSW's global brand reputation, create sharper brand distinctiveness, amplify UNSW's globally renowned expertise across Societal Impact Framework themes, and support the University to realise the ambition of its 'Progress for All' strategic plan.

Striving to be at the forefront of marketing and digital experience leading practice, M&DX instils audience centricity and leverages UNSW's enterprise technology investment, including but not limited to Adobe Experience Platform, Sales CRM, and emerging AI capabilities, to deliver deeper personalised relationships through sophisticated marketing, content, data, digital experience, and student recruitment functions in support of UNSW's commercial and societal impact outcomes.

As the Manager, Customer Analytics Transformation you will step into this newly created role where you will focus on the support of UNSW's lead management ecosystem and 'Progress for All' strategic ambitions. You will primarily be responsible for data strategy and governance, digital innovation and adoption, and benefit realisation to drive transformational enterprise-wide customer experience outcomes.

About You:  

This role will suit a strategic and forward-thinking leader who can drive the evolution of UNSW’s customer analytics capability, leveraging data, AI and digital innovation to deliver personalised, insight-driven experiences across the student, alumni and research lifecycle. More specifically you must have:

Provide operational leadership and manage the Customer Analytics transformation and innovation area and its team members.
Position M&DX as the Centre of Excellence for lead management and digital personalisation. Working collaboratively with marketing, sales and digital experience leads on the development and implementation of UNSW's data-and-technology-driven CX/DX innovation strategy and roadmap in alignment with 'Progress for All' and M&DX priorities.
Responsible for defining, developing and implementing the M&DX customer data and AI strategy in alignment with Enterprise Data Office (EDO) and UNSW IT principles and guardrails. This encompasses processes, guidelines, tooling, prioritisation and governance.
Product owner for RT-CDP and CJA. Responsible for the business configuration, sourcing and ingestion strategy, customer schema maintenance, consent management, customer segmentation, predictive analytics, business intelligence, reporting and insight generation.
Responsible for development and maintenance of M&DX AI models, governance and architecture. Responsible for establishing internal governance for data application deployment in coordination with UNSW IT and EDO – including security protocols, anomaly detection, incident management and monitoring, review cycles and effectiveness reporting.
Identify and facilitate prioritisation and development of high-value use cases for the customer analytics management ecosystem that demonstrate tangible business outcomes across student recruitment, alumni engagement, and research partnerships.
Partner on the strategic roadmap definition for lead management transformation, conducting horizon scanning to identify emerging trends and technologies that position UNSW as a sector leader.
Provide thought leadership to M&DX Leadership on digital analytics transformation, AI, operating model evolution, and capability development, particularly relating to Customer Data Management, Journey Orchestration, Sales Engagement, and AI-enabled technologies.
Enable customer journey strategies across all MDX priority audiences, driving the evolution from campaign-based to always-on lifecycle marketing approaches.
Foster innovation culture through experimentation and test-and-learn approaches, mentoring M&DX team members on customer analytics best practice.
Build and maintain a working relationship with UNSW stakeholders to advocate for customer-centric approaches and to ensure alignment and seamless integration.

For further details on role responsibilities and skills & experience required, refer to the position description found under the position listing on Jobs@UNSW

Benefits and Culture:

People are at the core of everything we do. We recognise it is the contributions of our staff who make UNSW one of the best universities in Australia and the world.

Our benefits include:

Flexible working options.
Career development opportunities.
17% Superannuation contributions and additional leave loading payments.
Additional 3 days of leave over Christmas period.
Discounts and entitlements (retail, education, fitness).

For further details on the benefits, please visit https://www.jobs.unsw.edu.au/lifestyle-benefits

Pre-Employment Checks
Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity, preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for.

These pre-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks:- 

National and International Criminal history checks
Entitlement to work and ID checks
Working With Children Checks
Completion of a Gender-Based Violence Prevention Declaration
Verification of relevant qualifications
Verification of relevant professional membership
Employment history and reference checks
Financial responsibility assessments/checks.
Medical Checks and Assessments

Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW.

How to Apply: Submit your CV & cover letter addressing the key selection criteria (no more than 2 pages) before 11.55pm Monday 6th April 2026. Please note: Sponsorship is not available for this role, valid Australian working rights are required on application.

Job queries contact: Chloe Gane, Talent Acquisition Consultant E: chloe.gane@unsw.edu.au  (applications to the address cannot be accepted, please apply online)

UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. Applications from women, people of culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those living with disabilities, members of the LGBTIQ+ community; and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, are encouraged. About Us

UniSuper is a workplace where highly skilled professionals are empowered to Think Great and deliver exceptional retirement outcomes for our members. Are you ready to take on new challenges, drive excellence and shape UniSuper's success? Join UniSuper as a leader and empower your team within an industry leading culture, driving simplification and performance.

The Technology & Delivery team ensures the sustained health and maintenance of UniSuper's core systems and platforms and partners with the business to plan and deliver technology change that supports scale, simplification and execution of UniSuper's strategy.

This is a pivotal leadership role where you will shape how change is planned, governed and delivered across UniSuper, enabling better decisions, stronger execution and meaningful outcomes for our members.

The role

The key focus of this role is enabling successful portfolio execution across Technology & Delivery through clear governance, insight and delivery support. You will provide portfolio‑level visibility, decision support and operating rhythm while continuously improving how UniSuper plans, funds and governs change. You will lead the evolution from a traditional PMO to a modern, enabling and outcome‑focused enterprise portfolio function.

You will be accountable for:
Shaping and leading the enterprise portfolio operating model to support effective planning, funding and delivery of change
Providing portfolio‑level oversight, insights and assurance across programs and projects, including risks, dependencies, progress and benefits
Leading and developing a high‑performing team that enables delivery excellence across Technology & Delivery
Establishing and overseeing fit‑for‑purpose governance that balances regulatory, financial and risk obligations with pace and agility
Partnering closely with the Chief Technology Officer and senior leaders to support decision making, prioritisation and execution
Overseeing portfolio financials, forecasting and benefit tracking to ensure alignment to strategic priorities

About you

You are a modern enterprise portfolio leader who brings strong experience in traditional program management and enjoys reshaping those practices into contemporary delivery models. You are comfortable operating at a portfolio and enterprise level, influencing senior leaders and creating clarity in complex environments. You value outcomes, transparency and continuous improvement and take pride in enabling others to deliver their best work.

You bring:


Proven experience leading an enterprise portfolio or Project Management Office in a complex organisation
Strong leadership and stakeholder management capability, with the ability to coach and influence at senior levels
Experience enabling modern delivery practices, governance and portfolio planning cadences
Sound financial and commercial acumen, including oversight of budgets and forecasting
Strong communication, analytical and systems thinking skills
A result focused, accountable and outcome driven mindset

What We Offer

UniSuper believes that the best way to achieve great things is when we come together and collaborate. Therefore, we ask you to be able to commit to 60% of your time in office. UniSuper is proud of our culture and benefits, which empower our people to achieve their full potential, thrive, and grow their career with us. These include:


Flexible working arrangements
Annual Bonus and Competitive Remuneration
Strong focus on learning, development and leadership growth
Competitive superannuation up to 17%
Wellbeing initiatives and support

It should go without saying, but at UniSuper, we value and celebrate diversity and inclusion. We believe that a variety of perspectives, backgrounds, interests, abilities, and skills is crucial for delivering great retirement outcomes for our members. We invite you to apply for the roles that suit your career aspirations, even if you don't meet all the requirements.

If you feel you have the experience and skills required for this role, please apply.


Think Great. Create Great.

High-growth services business

Sydney-based with international operations
Broad portfolio of growth and transformation projects

About the Company

This is a fast moving and ambitious services business which has grown successfully over many years and is now embarking on the next phase of development. With an experienced leadership team, they have multiple projects underway that will set them up for substantial growth over the next few years. Therefore this role will be a key member of a small senior team who can add value across the business by leveraging their broad project planning and execution skills and embedding that discipline across the organisation.

About the Role

This will be the first dedicated Project Manager role and will oversee the end-to-end delivery of varied cross-functional initiatives across this rapidly scaling business. This is a hands-on role suited to someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, enjoys solving complex problems, and can bring structure and clarity to ambiguous situations. You will work closely with teams across Product, Technology, Operations, Finance, Legal and Sales to plan, coordinate and successfully deliver key initiatives. Reporting to the CFO, you’ll play an important role in improving how the business delivers change — introducing practical project management disciplines and frameworks while ensuring teams remain aligned, accountable and focused on outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Project Planning & Execution

Lead the planning and delivery of cross-functional initiatives such as  technology projects, business integration and process improvements
Define project scope, milestones, timelines and success measures with stakeholders
Identify and manage risks, issues and dependencies
Ensure projects are delivered on time and aligned with business goals
Stakeholder Engagement

Work closely with leaders across the business to drive collaboration and alignment
Communicate project progress, risks and decisions clearly to stakeholders, including senior leaders
Manage competing priorities and negotiate realistic delivery timelines
Reporting

Introduce practical project management practices and governance to support effective delivery
Maintain clear documentation, reporting and decision tracking
Implement appropriate PM tools and methodologies
Resource & Budget Management

Determine resource requirements and allocate across projects appropriately
Monitor budgets and staffing plans
Work closely with CFO and finance team on forecast costs and financial accountability
  
About You

You will be comfortable working across different cultures in overseas offices and can quickly adjust to changing priorities. You should be pragmatic, collaborative and confident bringing structure to varied projects, while maintaining a strong focus on delivery outcomes.

Several years of experience in project management or a similar role delivering cross-functional initiatives
Experience working in large organisations but looking for a broader role in a growing, mid-size business
Proven ability to manage multi-million dollar budgets and perform financial analysis
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
Comfortable bringing structure to ambiguous or evolving environments
Strong command of project management tools and methodologies
Project and Change Management certifications (PRINCE2, PMP, Agile/Scrum, Six Sigma or similar)

Employment Type: Permanent Full Time
Position Classification: Health Mgr Lvl 4
Remuneration: $147,653 - $175,917 (plus superannuation and leave loading)
Hours Per Week: 38
Requisition ID: REQ649279
 

          

About the opportunity
In this role, you will lead an Economic Advisory service responsible for delivering high-quality Cost-Benefit Analysis (CBA) and Financial Analysis (including Financial Impact Statements and Financial Appraisals) to inform business cases for major health capital projects. You will work in close partnerships with Health Infrastructure Regions (project teams), NSW Health Entities, and a broad network of internal and external subject matter experts to ensure robust, evidence-based investment decisions.

As a senior leader, you will:
-    Lead and continuously enhance HI’s economic advisory framework, including the CBA Toolkit, methodologies, templates, and public-facing guidance.
-    Provide strategic advice to shape NSW Health financial advisory frameworks, with a particular focus on strengthening the implementation of the Financial Impact Statement templates and guidance
-    Embed continuous improvement through evaluations and assurance, contributing to post-project reviews and translating insights into improved practices

This is a full-time permanent role. Remuneration will be determined with the successful candidate.

Please note that as Health Infrastructure is an organisation of NSW Health, the successful applicant must reside in NSW or be willing to relocate if appointed.

This recruitment may be used to create a Talent Pool for similar future roles (ongoing or temporary) that may arise over the next 18 months. 

About you
You are an experienced economic and financial analyst with a strong track record in delivering rigorous, policy-aligned analysis to inform complex investment decisions. You combine technical expertise with strategic insight and can translate detailed modelling into clear, decision-ready advice.

You will bring:
•    Deep experience in CBA and financial analysis, ideally within government or advisory settings 
•    Strong understanding of business case and evaluation frameworks (e.g. NSW Treasury or equivalent) 
•    Advanced analytical, modelling and writing skills 
•    Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders in complex environments 
•    Leadership capability, including mentoring and driving improvements in frameworks and methods

You operate with autonomy, manage competing priorities effectively, and are motivated to deliver high-quality outcomes for the health system and community.

About us
Health Infrastructure delivers sustainable and innovative infrastructure solutions to meet the health care needs of NSW communities now and into the future. We collaborate with government and industry to transform public health facilities across NSW. Outside of health we contribute longer term benefits to NSW communities including the creation of jobs and increased opportunities for local businesses.

About your Role

The Data Governance Manager is accountable for strengthening TMBL’s Data Governance position by assisting with the uplifting and embedding of a fit-for-purpose Data Governance framework across the organisation, including governance, policies, training, reporting and assurance. You will support the strategic development and implementation of data solutions that address regulatory requirements including Economic and Financial Statistics (EFS) reporting and Open Banking compliance.
  
You will support the Bank’s data governance agenda, ensuring robust management of critical data elements (CDEs), clear data ownership, documented data lineage, and effective data retention practices. You will act as an escalation point for Data Governance issues and incident management processes and monitor and support the implementation of Data Governance-related regulatory changes and best practices across TMBL.

Key Responsibilities

Provide timely, high-quality, risk-based Data Governance advice on complex matters, including technology change and AI initiatives.
Collaborate with enterprise risk and compliance teams to align regulatory reporting with broader risk management frameworks and support the Risk team through reporting and analytical solutions.
Act as an escalation point to assess Data Governance incidents, coordinate investigation and response, and support regulatory notifications.
Operationalise and continuously improve TMBL’s Data Governance framework, driving pragmatic governance, reporting, assurance and continuous compliance to drive data governance framework uplift.
Develop/maintain Data Governance policies, standards, procedures and Regulatory change oversight: Track upcoming Data Governance regulatory changes, translate obligations into business requirements, and support implementation and uplift activity.
Data Quality & Assurance – Drive initiatives to monitor and improve the quality, integrity, and defensibility of regulatory and risk-related data.
Training & culture: Design and deliver Data Governance training and communications to build capability and embed a strong Data Governance culture.
About You

As the successful  candidate you will showcase: 

Demonstrated experience in Data Governance roles within financial services, including managing critical data elements (CDEs), data lineage, ownership, and retention.
Background in data quality management and assurance, especially in highly regulated reporting environments.
Strong stakeholder management and partnership experience across cross-functional teams (e.g., Risk, Compliance, Technology, Customer, Legal, Cyber, Privacy, Product, Operations) to deliver integrated data solutions.
Experience in developing and/or updating Data Governance Policies, Standards & Procedures.
Hands-on experience designing and implementing data systems and solutions that meet compliance and reporting standards.
Experience with Data Management tooling such as Alation, Collibra or similar.
Working knowledge of CPG235, BCBS239, and CPS230.
  
Skills and Qualifications
  

Relevant Tertiary Qualification in Commerce, Economics, Finance, Computer Science or Similar
Strong and extensive experience in a similar data governance management  role working within a major Australian Bank
Professional Certification in Data Governance DCAM- must have
Professional certifications in data governance compliance or risk management (e.g., DCAM, DAMA-DMBOK, CISA, CRISC)- highly desirable.
Training or experience in data governance, risk/compliance, technology, cyber, or AI governance.
  
About Us

Teachers Mutual Bank Limited is one of the largest mutual banks in Australia. We are a globally recognised socially responsible bank with profit-for-purpose as our business model and philosophy. At our Bank, social responsibility is built-in, not bolted on and drives our business practices, people, and products. We are proud to serve some of Australia’s essential workers in education, emergency services and healthcare. We believe they deserve a bank that does good for their community.
  
We have been named one of the World’s Most Ethical Companies for ten years in a row and are a Certified B Corp Bank.

Our employees embrace our organisation’s values of passion, advocacy, and sustainability, and contribute to a diverse and inclusive workplace culture founded on mutual respect, equality, and a focus on striving to deliver exceptional member service.

Job Title: Integrated Product Team Agile Product Owner

Ad Text:
About us:

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation.  As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world’s most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.

AIR6500 will provide a Joint Air Battle Management System capability for the Royal Australian Air Force. The system will provide multi-domain integration of air, electromagnetic, and missile defence battle management including deployable & fixed operations centres, communications, and both active & passive sensors.

Responsibilities

The Product Owner’s primarily responsibilities include:

·         Liaising between Scrum Team and Commonwealth Customer, ensuring Epics, Features, and User Stories are understood and reflect customer needs and values

·         Maximising the value of the products created by the IPT through communicating the vision and managing the product’s backlog,

·         Inspecting, evaluating and planning product progress through each iteration,

·         Actively participating in team sprint planning and proactively raise risk/Issues with IPT Lead/ Product manager,

·         Working cross functionally within IPT teams to unblock the Mission issues and contribute to business success, and

·         Actively contributing to PI planning and assisting teams to structure the features for Epic delivery.

·         Responsible for the prioritisation, accuracy, and completeness of the teams Product Backlog Items (PBI’s), acceptance criteria, and nonfunctional requirements, and the Team backlog is aligned with the ART backlog priorities and is sustainable,

·         Aligns with the ART Product Manager’s vision and roadmap,

·         Responsible for understanding, capturing, and translating customer requirements and value,

·         Accepts the work of the team during the iteration, ensures the work is complete, meets the acceptance criteria, and seeks feedback from the customer,

·         Responsible for setting and communicating the Sprint / Iteration Goal and ensuring it aligns with the Vision, Mission, and Goal of the PI,

·         Ensure the integrity of engineering activity by employing both quantitative and qualitative management approaches. Collect and use data to make data-driven decisions,

·         Responsible for the continual identification and active management of teams’ risks, technical debt, and opportunities,

·         Foster an inclusive work environment that values diversity, sharing, out-of-the-box thinking and seeing the big-picture, and help the Scrum Master create a psychologically safe environment for all development team members.





About you:

To be successful in this role you will also have:

·         Significant experience with Agile Product Development leveraging Scrum & Kanban and Scaled Agile Framework, including backlog management and writing user stories.

·         Significant understanding of the Software Development Lifecycle.

·         Significant experience with software engineering methodologies, systems engineering processes, DevSecOps and the overall engineering product lifecycle, preferably in a web-based development domain.

·         Significant experience in leading multiple team and coordinating with team leads for successful outcomes.

·         Significant experience with digital transformation campaigns, promoting the adoption of transformative tooling, processes and practices.

·         Significant experience presenting complex solutions to audiences of wide-ranging knowledge levels.

·         Significant understanding of program, functional, and process metrics including Earned Value Management (EVM).

·         Experience with Cyber security and Governance Frameworks (ISM).

·         Experience leading cross functional teams in Agile environment for successful outcome. Desirable

·         Control Account Management (CAM) experience.

·         SAFe or other recognized Agile framework Certification.

·         Experience in Systems Engineering, Complex Systems Integration & Test, Model Based Systems Development (MBSD) and ModelBased Engineering (MBE) processes, principles and tools.

·         Experience leading multiple teams through change.

·         Knowledge and experience with IBM CLM/ELM tool suite (DOORS Next, Rhapsody, ETM, etc).

How we reward and support you:

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

·         Flexible work & Hybrid work options

·         Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

·         A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

·         Additional benefits such as Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs

·         Learning & Development platforms to encourage ongoing self-development

Special Requirements:

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

A Veteran-friendly Workplace:

Veterans are encouraged to apply. We highly value the unique skills and experiences that veterans and transitioning service members can contribute to our team. We are proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Australian Government.

Equal Opportunity Statement:

Australia:

Lockheed Martin Australia supports Equal Employment Opportunity and values diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, free of unlawful discrimination and is harassment free, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. By leveraging our employees’ unique talents and experiences, we will deliver innovative, affordable solutions and unparalleled customer value.

As part of our endeavours to increase participation of Indigenous Australians, we welcome applications from all people, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are selected for this role, we would be happy to discuss any accommodations that will help you to be successful.

Ad Text:
Join a high-profile transformation initiative within a leading Queensland organisation and play a pivotal role in delivering a knowledge management uplift that will directly impact frontline teams and customer outcomes.
This is not your typical technology project. This is a people-first transformation, focused on how knowledge is created, shared, and used across the business.

The Opportunity:
You will step into an in-flight project that sits at the heart of a broader enterprise transformation program. With the platform already implemented, the focus now shifts to content, adoption, and embedding sustainable ways of working. You’ll lead the delivery of a Knowledge Management project that will:

For more information please reach out to Max at mpower@sharpandcarter.com.au

Join Us at a Defining Moment

At Intelligen, we’re building something different. Over the past three years, we’ve evolved from a bold startup to a trusted partner for some of Australia’s most complex data transformations. We’ve helped organisations move from legacy to modern platforms, embedded governance into decision-making, and brought AI into the hands of the business -responsibly and at speed.

But we’re just getting started. As we scale into our next phase of growth, we’re looking for senior, seasoned consultants who don’t just want to deliver great solutions but help shape the future of data, governance, and AI capability.


Our missing person...

We’re looking for a Data Governance (Senior Consultant) who can help our clients turn governance into a genuine business enabler across data, analytics, and AI. You’ll work across people, process, and technology to design, implement, and sustain governance programs that improve trust in data, strengthen compliance, and enable responsible AI adoption.

This role is perfect for someone who thrives at the intersection of strategy and delivery - comfortable advising executives one moment and working hands-on with data teams the next.

You’ll be highly client-facing in this role, acting as a trusted advisor who can challenge constructively, push back where needed, and help clients make pragmatic governance decisions that balance control with delivery.

This role also requires a strong coordinator who can bring together business, data and risk stakeholders to drive alignment, maintain momentum, and ensure governance initiatives translate into practical outcomes.

Key Responsibilities
Design and implement enterprise data governance frameworks, policies, standards, and operating models aligned to client goals and industry best practice.
Define the Enterprise Data Governance model and operating rhythm to underpin AI, analytics, and broader data transformation initiatives.
Establish minimum standards for AI-enabling data, including data quality, lineage, metadata, access, and accountability.
Map AI governance requirements to existing data governance artefacts, controls, and business processes.
Contribute to the AI Governance Framework and Operating Model, ensuring alignment between responsible AI practices and enterprise data foundations.
Define and document data ownership, stewardship, and accountability models across business and technology functions.
Provide strong day-to-day coordination across client stakeholders, ensuring governance decisions, actions, and dependencies are managed effectively across business and technical teams.
Support the establishment of data quality management processes, metrics, controls, and monitoring mechanisms.
Facilitate workshops, stakeholder forums, and training sessions to drive adoption, engagement, and cultural change around data ownership, literacy, and responsible AI.
Collaborate with client leadership to improve decision-making, regulatory compliance, business performance, and confidence in AI and analytics outcomes.


Requirements



6+ years’ experience in data governance, data management, information management, or consulting roles.
Experience developing and implementing data governance policies and procedures within complex organisational environments.
Strong understanding of data governance principles, frameworks, and best practices, including familiarity with DAMA-DMBOK and related governance disciplines.
Proven experience designing or implementing governance frameworks, policies, standards, stewardship models, and operating models.
Experience supporting AI and digital transformation initiatives through strong data governance foundations.
Strong understanding of the foundational data capabilities required to enable AI, including data quality, metadata, lineage, access controls, and governance by design.
Experience mapping governance requirements across business, risk, compliance, and technology stakeholders.
Familiarity with metadata management, data cataloguing, and lineage tools such as Collibra, Informatica, Alation, or similar platforms.
Exposure to data quality management processes, controls, and tooling.
Strong consulting and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to advise senior leaders while also working closely with delivery teams.
Excellent communication and facilitation skills - able to translate governance concepts into practical business value for both executive and operational audiences.
Comfortable working in multi-disciplinary teams spanning strategy, engineering, analytics, and AI.


Benefits

About the Role

The Improvement & Analytics team within Whitehaven's Remote Operating Centre (ROC) in Brisbane has an exciting opportunity for a Specialist - Data Analytics to play a key role in shaping the future of our operations.

This position places you at the forefront of innovation in mining, directly influencing how our assets operate today and into the future.
You will have opportunities to expand your technical capability, work on complex challenges, and contribute to a high‑performing team focused on excellence, innovation, and continuous improvement.

Reporting to the Superintendent - Improvement & Analytics, you will support our operating assets in making informed, data‑driven decisions through ongoing collaboration, maintaining the integrity of production data, and optimising key processes across the value chain.


About Whitehaven's Remote Operating Centre (ROC)

Whitehaven's Brisbane‑based ROC is a major driver of our digital transformation and modern mining strategy.
The ROC integrates advanced operational technology, real‑time data, and innovative digital tools to optimise the performance of our mining operations.

Here, teams work with high‑quality data streams, market leading analytics platforms, and emerging technologies to support safer, more efficient, and more predictable operations.
You will join a collaborative, high‑performance environment that leverages data, automation, and innovation to deliver continuous improvement across our business.
The ROC offers a unique opportunity to influence the future of mining by shaping how intelligence, insights, and digital capability are applied across our operations.



What You Will Do

• Validate data quality and develop innovative solutions to reduce the administrative effort associated with manual reconciliation.
• Develop dashboards, visualisations, and actionable insights using tools such as SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), Power BI, PI Vision, SSRS, and related platforms.
• Build digital products using Fabric, Power Automate and other Microsoft 365 tools.
• Create and maintain clear documentation for data workflows to support consistent and efficient processes within the Data & Analytics team.
• Act as a liaison between on‑site operations and the Data & Analytics team, providing insights and resolving data‑related queries to enable informed decision‑making.

What You Will Bring

• Intermediate understanding of key mining processes and the mining value chain.
• Experience with analysing and interpreting data from fleet management systems and washplant source systems.
• Demonstrated advanced proficiency in SQL, DAX, Power BI, and related languages

• Demonstrated experience with data reconciliation, reporting, and document management.
• Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability, with the ability to draw meaningful insights from data.
• Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with both on‑site and off‑site stakeholders.

Highly Regarded Skills

Candidates with experience or interest in the following areas will be viewed favourably, as these capabilities align with the ongoing digital and analytical evolution of our ROC:



• Data Science - statistical analysis, optimisation, data modelling, or analytics‑driven process improvement.
• Data Engineering - ETL processes, data pipelines, cloud data platforms, or database optimisation.
• Automation & Scripting - Python, R, or automation tools to reduce manual work and streamline data processes.
• Power BI & Visual Analytics - development of dashboards, visualisations, and insights for a range of stakeholders.
• Operational Technology (OT) Data - experience with sensor data, real‑time systems, historian databases (e.g., PI), or telemetry‑based monitoring.
• Process Optimisation - exposure to Lean, Six Sigma, or continuous improvement frameworks supported by data‑driven insights.

A rare opportunity to sit at the centre of large‑scale settlement scheme delivery

Direct access to partners and senior decision‑makers

An entrepreneurial, low‑hierarchy environment

A role with genuine scope to grow into broader scheme administration leadership

Competitive remuneration commensurate with experience

Deloitte's UKG practice is the largest in Australia and continues to grow rapidly. Our team brings together specialists who design and deliver workforce technology solutions that help organisations better manage, schedule and support their people.

You'll combine deep product expertise with consulting capability to design and deliver workforce technology solutions that integrate seamlessly within a client's broader HR and payroll technology landscape.

Facilitating client workshops to capture and translate workforce management requirements

Staying up to date with the latest UKG releases, innovations and certifications

Our work combines technology, business insight and practical delivery to help organisations transform how they manage and support their workforce.

AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner.  AEMO is embarking on a technology uplift program, designed to modernise systems, enhance operational efficiency, and shape the future of the energy landscape. This large-scale transformation is an exciting opportunity to join a high-profile initiative and contribute to its success from the ground up.

 The Benefits Realisation Lead role is responsible for measuring and tracking agreed outcomes to ensure the program’s goals translate into measurable outcomes. You’ll monitor progress, report on benefits and influence change management across a complex, multi-disciplinary environment. This role will track success measures and validate achievements against Program milestones that will deliver a significant risk reduction Program of national significance.

 Position Responsibilities

The Benefits Realisation Lead is responsible for ensuring all the ‘critical project’ benefits are clearly defined, accurately tracked, and transparently reported in alignment with the Commonwealth Grant Agreement. Working closely with the Program Director, Finance Business Partner, PMO Manager, Program Manager, and Workstream Leads, this role provides end‑to‑end oversight of benefits management across the Program. Maintaining and reporting on the project Benefits Realisation Management Plan, ensuring the documentation remains current and aligned with the Program requirements

Defining frameworks for tracking and reporting project outcomes against benefits.  Conducting pre-and-post implementation evaluations and lessons learnt exercises for benefits reporting and management.

Co-ordination with Program Manager, Workstream leads and Benefits Owners to ensure accurate understanding of the workstreams/projects' contribution to associated benefits and sub-benefits.

Supporting Workstream Leads in completing benefits reporting through the Benefits Register and related mechanisms.

Reviewing benefit updates via the benefit dashboards and preparing monthly benefits reports, highlighting risks to benefit realisation and recommending mitigation actions where required.

Providing input into scope changes and assessing their impact on proposed benefits. Operating within established governance arrangements to ensure robust project management and integration of benefits management.

Preparing high-quality monthly benefit reports, briefing notes, presentations, and progress updates for leadership teams.

Co-ordination with Workstream leads, Project Managers and Benefit Owners to identify new or emergent benefits that warrant tracking. .

About the program:

AIR6500 is the RAAF’s $3 billion program to develop a joint air battle management system that builds on the capabilities offered by Next Generation sensor and command and control systems. AIR6500 will create a fully networked ‘system of systems’ that synchronises air and missile defence operations in joint and coalition environments, maximising situational awareness and enhancing the speed of decision making across the battlespace.





About the role:

We are seeking an experienced Logistics Support Analysis Lead to join the AIR6500 team, supporting the delivery of Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) and Logistics Support Analysis (LSA) activities within an Agile program environment.

In this role, you will work closely with the ILS Lead and Systems Engineering, Integration and Test (SEIT) Team, to:

Plan and lead the team to execute Logistics Support Analysis (LSA) work packages within the Program’s Agile framework.

Develop technical LSA studies and evaluations of engineering design concepts.

Lead and Mentor the team tasked to capture LSA data in associated templates and databases.

Develop and compile plans, studies and reports as required contractually.

Lead workshops with internal and external stakeholders to define and tailor the LSA program.

Ensure compliance with standards, certification and assurance to support system design certification.

Serve as SME on LSA activities, providing advice and guidance to internal and external stakeholders.

 

About you:

You are an experienced LSA professional who has a deep knowledge of stakeholder management and ability to identify, analyse, select, document and design for all LSA requirements. You bring:

Deep knowledge of ILS process, procedures and the overall engineering product lifecycle.

Defence Industry Project delivery experience including, specialist technical application of Integrated Logistics Support including Logistics Support Analysis (LSA) tasks, sparing, maintenance support, training and documentation to engineering projects.

Extensive experience with supportability analysis, MRD, and Repairable Item Management.

Leadership of large teams to deliver successful outcomes.

Effective technical guidance, mentoring and training to other employees.

Deep understanding of working with and leading contractual processes and deliverables.


Desirable:

Knowledge of adjacent ILS disciplines including training and publications.

Experience using tools such as MILIS/ERP, CAMM2, OPUS10, Eagle or similar logistics/reliability systems.

Experience in Agile development application.


Why join us:

Contribute to a nationally critical Joint Air Battle Management capability

Work within a complex, multi-domain Defence system-of-systems program

Collaborate with Australian and US teams on advanced Defence technologies

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

Flexible work & Hybrid work options

Reporting to the Lead Analyst within the Economic & Health Facility Advisory team, you will provide expert evaluation advice, support post-completion evaluation activities and embed a culture of evidence-based planning across project teams. You are experienced in developing and maintaining productive relationships with key stakeholders, including senior executives and cross-agency partners, and can influence to achieve intended outcomes. The opportunity

As a Manager in the Finance Consulting team, you will play a pivotal role in helping clients navigate complex, time‑critical finance challenges and own delivery end‑to‑end, from mobilisation through to final recommendations and outcomes.

You will lead multi‑workstream finance engagements, partnering closely with CFOs and senior finance leaders to provide clear judgement, commercial insight, and pragmatic solutions. Your work will span strategic, operational, and digital finance transformation, including the design and implementation of future‑ready finance operating models, enterprise performance improvement, and technology‑enabled transformation, including the integration of Agentic AI into Finance functions.

This role is based in Warrang (Sydney) and offers the opportunity to sit at the centre of delivery, decision‑making, and people leadership in one of the world's leading professional services firms.

Your key responsibilities

Lead complex finance transformation projects involving digital strategy, process optimisation, future-state operating model design, and the implementation of enabling technologies such as ERP (SAP, Microsoft Dynamics etc), EPM, AI, RPA, and Power BI. Engage directly with CFOs, GMs, and senior finance leaders, leading senior‑level conversations, framing decisions, and clearly articulating implications, trade‑offs, and the "so what".
Inspire and guide high-performing teams by mentoring junior consultants, collaborating across service lines, and delivering integrated solutions that drive sustainable change.
Provide strategic insights, and support business development through proposal leadership, solution design, and thought leadership, with a strong focus on commercial judgement and delivery credibility.

Skills and attributes for success

4-8 years of experience in finance transformation, consulting, or in-house finance roles, ideally within professional services, Big 4, or industry finance teams.
Strong knowledge of core finance functions (e.g. FP&A, record-to-report, order-to-cash), with hands-on experience in finance strategy, process improvement, and system implementations.
Proven ability to lead and influence with strong communication, stakeholder management, and problem-solving skills; experience with tools like SAP, Microsoft, Blackline, or Tagetik is a plus. One of the sectors we work with is the Australian Federal Government and as such Australian Citizenship is preferred.

Ideally, you'll also have the skills and attributes below but don't worry if you don't tick all the boxes. We're interested in your aptitude, attitude and willingness to learn.

Relevant qualifications such as CA, CPA, CFA, or MBA
A finance related degree

Collaborate with Marketing to ensure submissions meet brand standards, utilise approved templates, and incorporate high‑quality design elements.
5. Content Strategy & Continuous Improvement

Own and evolve all tender content, case studies, and standard responses within AI-enabled platforms.
Analyse tender outcomes and feedback to identify trends, gaps, and opportunities for improvement.
Drive continuous improvement in bid quality, efficiency, and effectiveness.
6. Governance & Delivery

Manage multiple tenders concurrently, prioritising workloads and meeting tight deadlines.
Ensure all submissions comply with brand, governance, risk, and regulatory requirements.

Guiding the future of NSW education 

About the role


The Lead, Measurement and Research Services is a senior quantitative leadership role within NESA’s Assessment Division, responsible for supporting NSW-wide assessment programs, including the Higher School Certificate (HSC), the HSC Minimum Standards Tests, and NAPLAN.

The role involves computational modelling to translate policy and assessment frameworks into precise mathematical and statistical formulations, supported by intensive analytical coding in R. It requires strong quantitative judgement to design, implement, and evaluate models that underpin high-stakes decisions, often under tight time constraints.

You will work across a wide range of analytical problems, including complex modelling, large-scale data analysis, model evaluation, and analytical quality assurance to ensure outputs are robust, defensible, and fit for purpose. In addition to hands-on technical work, the role provides leadership and guidance to a team of statisticians and data scientists, promoting high standards of analytical practice, reproducibility, and documentation.

A key component of the role is advising and communicating with senior internal stakeholders, governance bodies, and expert committees. You will present and explain complex quantitative findings to non-technical audiences, support policy deliberations, and contribute to decision-making processes where analytical rigour and clarity are critical.

This role offers an opportunity to apply deep quantitative problem-solving skills to work with real-world impact, influencing outcomes for students across NSW.

Why join NESA

Just some of the benefits of working with NESA include:

An organisation where your work has a meaningful impact on HSC and NAPLAN outcomes, supporting equity and excellence in education.
A prime CBD location (all modes of transport nearby, and excellent coffee and eateries) with refurbished offices.
Flexible working arrangements and generous leave entitlements with standard working hours 35 hours per week.
Accrue flex time for hours worked over the standard hours and enjoy true work-life balance.
A range of leave to support you with your needs out of work including generous paid parental leave, carers’ leave and more.
Access to discounted health and fitness memberships via Fitness Passport, an employee assistance program and annual flu vaccinations.
Salary packaging options.
Working in a purpose driven and ethical organisation with committed colleagues.

 

Position details
Clerk Grade 11/12
Full-Time, Ongoing
Close to Wynyard station & hybrid work arrangements available 

About us


At the NSW Education Standards Authority (NESA), we are dedicated to carrying out meaningful work that drives improvements and elevates student achievement across NSW, now and into the future.

We accomplish this by supporting all school sectors with high-quality syllabuses, assessment (including managing the HSC and NAPLAN), teaching standards (e.g., accrediting teachers) and school environments (including setting and monitoring school standards).

NESA is a unique organisation in NSW (of around 740 FTE staff) with significant state-wide impact, visit our website to learn more about the important work we do.


On a day-to-day basis you will be responsible for: 
Leading the unit in delivering core functions, particularly during the HSC written examination period.
Solving complex and time-critical quantitative problems, including diagnosing and validating analytical logic in complex systems with limited documentation.
Designing and implementing robust and automated analytical workflows that generate high-stakes quantitative outputs for ministerial, executive, and statutory reporting.
Proactively identifying and responding to emerging issues to ensure effective resolution and minimise delivery and analytical risks.
Leading and contributing to projects that support strategic and operational objectives.
Providing expert quantitative advice to senior stakeholders, governance committees, and policy forums, including clear communication of complex technical issues to non-technical audiences.
Leading, mentoring, and providing technical oversight to statisticians and data scientists, including code and modelling review and capability development.
Exercising professional judgement in determining appropriate analytical approaches, modelling assumptions, and trade-offs in time-critical contexts to ensure outputs are accurate, defensible, and fit for purpose.

 

To be successful you will need:
PhD in Mathematics, Data Science, Statistics, Engineering, Physics, Computer Science or other quantitative science or equivalent professional experience demonstrating comparable depth in large-scale, high-stakes quantitative modelling and analysis.
Strong analytical thinking, problem‑solving ability and attention to detail.
Experience in data wrangling, scientific computing, modelling and visualising using R (primary) and Python.
Familiarity with version control systems (Git) and reproducible coding standards and ensuring consistent adoption of these practices across direct reports.
High-level written, verbal, and presentation skills, with the ability to communicate clearly and engage internal and external stakeholders.
Demonstrated experience preparing high-quality technical documentation using LaTeX, RMarkdown, or Quarto.
Willingness to work outside standard working hours during the HSC period (paid), with the ability to remain focused and work efficiently under high pressure. 
Demonstrated ability to lead teams in delivering fit-for-purpose, high-quality outcomes within business timeframes, making informed trade-offs where required.
Demonstrated commitment to cultivating a team culture that embraces ongoing learning in emerging technologies and modelling techniques.

 

It is desirable to have:
Experience in psychometric modelling, including knowledge of Item Response Theory.
Proficiency in TAM, dplyr and SQL.
Familiarity with Generative AI and AI-assisted or agentic coding tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot, Codex, Cursor), with the judgement to support their safe and appropriate use within the team.
Experience in leading and coaching data scientists and statisticians in a fast-paced environment, applying advanced statistical and, where appropriate, machine learning techniques to complex datasets. 
Experience using cloud-based platforms (e.g. AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud) and Linux environments to support scientific computing or large-scale analytical workloads.

Additionally, you'll play a key role in business planning for new stores, budgeting, forecasting and other ad hoc analysis when required.

You are an experienced Financial or Commercial Analyst - who will be ready to step into a Commercial Manager role in the next 1-2 years. Circa 5 years experience in Commercial Finance role/s would be ideal.

You'll have proven analytical skills, combined with a background in business partnering. You will also have advanced Excel / modelling skills, whilst experience using a BI tool and working with large data sets.

For more information, please contact Schenuk Wijesekera on 02 9775 5916. Or to apply, hit the "apply" button.

Job Title: Data Analyst - Multiple Positions - Tier 1 Bank
Ad Text:
We are looking for multiple Data Analyst's to join a high profile program of work in a leading Domestic bank, where you will have a broad audience across the business, technology, risk and finance.

As part of your role you'll work closely with stakeholders to understand and deliver the impact assessment of key projects inflight, how the business needs to adapt, and how to mitigate any undue risk to customers, all from the lens of data and data impacts.

You'll be responsible for:

Gap analysis and recommend new data flows that need to be implemented

Business case analysis through extensive data extraction and interrogation

Develop ETL packages and intuitive PowerBI dashboards for the business

Integrate yourself into agile delivery squads and ensure data requirements are captured

Review data designs and guide technology on data lifecycle management 

You'll play a key role in this transformation team as lead data resource when it comes to assesses the impact of programs of work on the business.

What sort of background do you need to be successful in this role?

Strong competency with SQL and data warehousing experience

Experience with reporting, data visualisation and storytelling insights with tools such as Tableau and Power BI

Experience in Banking/ Fin Services is mandatory

Principal Consumer Insurance (CI) Strategy

Permanent opportunity
Location Sydney (flexible working supported)

Join Suncorp’s Consumer Insurance team in a senior strategy leadership role with meaningful impact across one of Australia’s leading financial services organisations.

About the role

As Principal, Consumer Insurance (CI) Strategy, you will support the development of the Consumer Insurance strategy, ensuring alignment with Group strategy and enterprise priorities. Reporting to the Executive Manager, Consumer Insurance Strategy, this role plays a critical advisory and leadership position, partnering closely with senior leaders to solve complex strategic problems, shape long-term direction, and deliver high-quality insights to Executive Leadership and Boards.

This is a First Line Leader role at Fixed Salary Band 6, offering exposure to senior executives and the opportunity to influence outcomes at scale.

More specifically, you will:

Strategy development

Support the development of the Consumer Insurance strategy in close collaboration with senior leaders, ensuring it is clear, actionable and aligned with Group strategy
Define problem statements and draft proposals to scope activities, milestones, deliverables and engagement model
Lead hypothesis-driven, first-principles problem solving across a wide range of strategic issues
Facilitate strategy workshops, including off-sites, deep dives and ad hoc sessions
Deliver strategic research and insights, including market scans, competitor analysis, deep dives, opportunity assessments and commercial analyses
Build strong cross-functional relationships to ensure integration and alignment of strategic initiatives
Provide coaching and mentoring on junior members of the team (as required)

Business and strategic planning

Support Consumer Business Planning activities in line with Group Strategy milestones
Facilitate alignment between Consumer Insurance plans and broader enterprise objectives
Track progress against strategic milestones, identifying dependencies, risks and trade-offs
Provide clear, data-driven analysis and recommendations to support executive decision-making

Board and executive deliverables

Prepare and deliver high-quality presentations and reports for the Group and Consumer Boards, Executive Leadership Team (ELT), regulators and partners
Ensure timely, accurate and insightful content for key Board meetings, particularly May and November cycles
Clearly articulate strategic priorities, progress, risks and opportunities for Consumer Insurance

Ad hoc strategic analysis

Support additional strategic, commercial or market analysis as required

About you

You are a highly capable strategy professional with strong executive presence, commercial judgement and a passion for solving complex business problems.

Approximately 7+ years’ experience in corporate or business unit strategy, including some leadership experience
At least 5 years’ strategy consulting experience at a top-tier firm strategy house (preferred)
Insurance experience desirable but not essential
Demonstrated experience applying strategic frameworks in corporate or consulting environments
Strong hypothesis-driven and structured problem-solving capability
High level of commercial and financial acumen, including valuation concepts
Advanced analytical, quantitative and qualitative skills
Proven ability to influence and manage senior executive stakeholders
Ability to lead, inspire and collaborate with diverse professional teams
Expert-level written communication, including Board and executive-level materials
Confidence facilitating complex strategic conversations and workshops
Strong data-led decision-making capability

Why join us?

Suncorp supports flexibility in how, when and where work is done, where business needs permit. You’ll be joining a collaborative, purpose-led organisation where strategic thinking is valued and your work directly shapes outcomes for customers and the business.

What we can offer you:

Discounts and offers on a range of insurance products
A range of flexible working and leave options, including 20 weeks paid parental leave, 4 weeks secondary carer parental leave, and unlimited paid emergency response leave.
Invest in your brighter future with ongoing study support and career development programs.
Give back to our communities with payroll giving, donation matching and paid volunteer leave.
Prioritise your work/life balance with our robust employee assistance program and dedicated employee council.

Employee benefits | Suncorp Group

At Suncorp we believe we are our best when our workforce is as diverse, talented and passionate as the communities in which we live and operate, and where our people feel included, valued and connected. We are passionate about inspiring our people by creating a diverse, accessible, and inclusive culture, offering flexible work, career development and internal mobility, and building connected relationships amongst our team members and with our customers.

Key Responsibilities
Design, configure, and deliver SAP Finance solutions across FI/CO, including General Ledger, Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, and Fixed Assets.
Support SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Bluefield migrations, including selective data transition and hands‑on involvement in data transformation activities.
Analyse current ECC finance processes and structures and translate them into optimised S/4HANA Finance solutions.
Work closely with data migration teams to support financial data validation, reconciliation, and migration.
Collaborate with cross‑functional SAP teams across MM, SD, EAM, and PS to ensure end‑to‑end integration.
Support system integration testing (SIT), UAT, and financial reconciliation activities.
Drive finance process improvement aligned to S/4HANA best practices and Clean Core principles.
Contribute to cutover planning, go‑live activities, and post‑implementation hypercare support.
Required Skills & Experience
8+ years' experience working as an SAP Finance Functional Consultant.
Strong functional expertise across SAP FI/CO.
Hands‑on experience with GL, AP, AR, Fixed Asset Accounting, and indirect tax frameworks (GST/VAT).
Mandatory experience delivering SAP S/4HANA Bluefield migrations, including selective data transition.
Strong understanding of the S/4HANA Finance data model, including the Universal Journal (ACDOCA).
Proven experience supporting financial data migration, validation, and reconciliation.
Familiarity with SAP Activate methodology and S/4HANA best practices.
Ability to engage effectively with business stakeholders, technical teams, and external delivery partners.
Key Competencies
Strong understanding of end‑to‑end finance business processes.
Ability to translate complex business requirements into clear SAP functional solutions.
Excellent analytical, problem‑solving, and troubleshooting skills.
Experience working on large, complex SAP transformation programs.
Strong communication skills and confidence engaging with senior stakeholders.
Collaborative mindset with the ability to work effectively in cross‑functional SAP teams.
High‑quality documentation and solution design capability.
Proven focus on data integrity and financial accuracy during transformations.
Solid understanding of integrations between finance and logistics modules.
Ability to thrive in fast‑paced project environments and meet delivery milestones.

Why Join?

Join a flagship S/4HANA program, deepen Bluefield expertise, work on complex finance transformations, and accelerate your SAP consulting career.

Job Title: Solutions Engineer / Pre Sales Consultant (Risk & Audit)New
Ad Text:
Position Overview:

This role is ideal for someone from a Risk, Audit, or Compliance background who enjoys working closely with customers, solving complex problems, and presenting thoughtful, high-impact solutions in a commercial environment.

As a Solutions Engineer at Diligent, you will partner closely with Sales to help organisations understand how our platform can transform the way they manage risk, audit, and compliance. You’ll act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, translating risk and audit challenges into clear, compelling solutions that drive real business value.

If you enjoy combining domain expertise, storytelling, and customer interaction and want to move beyond traditional internal risk or audit roles, this is a strong next step.

Key Responsibilities:

Customer & Commercial Partnership


Partner closely with Sales to support the full pre-sales lifecycle, helping customers understand how Diligent solves real-world risk and audit challenges.

Deliver tailored, high-impact software demonstrations that clearly articulate business value not just product features.

Engage confidently with senior leaders across Audit, Risk, Compliance, IT, and Governance functions.

Translate customer pain points into clear solution narratives that support deal progression and closure.

Solution Design & Thought Leadership


Design and present solution architectures that align with customer objectives and regulatory environments.

Articulate the cross-platform value of the Diligent Platform and how it supports integrated GRC programs.

Collaborate with Product teams to provide customer and domain feedback on product enhancements.

Support marketing initiatives such as webinars, industry events, and thought-leadership content.

Domain Expertise


Apply practical knowledge of Audit, Risk Management, and IT Compliance in customer conversations.

Leverage familiarity with industry frameworks such as COSO ERM, ISO 31000, NIST RMF, and related standards.

Demonstrate how modern GRC platforms enable risk identification, assessment, monitoring, and mitigation at scale.

Required Skills/Experience

Experience in Risk, Audit, Compliance, or GRC, either in a customer-facing role or within industry.

Experience working with SaaS solutions or strong interest in moving into a commercial, customer-facing environment.

Strong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex topics.

Comfortable partnering with Sales and contributing to commercial outcomes.

Curious, self-driven, and motivated to continuously deepen both technical and domain knowledge.

#LI-SM1

#LI-Hybrid

About Us

Diligent is the AI leader in governance, risk and compliance (GRC) SaaS solutions, helping more than 1 million users and 700,000 board members to clarify risk and elevate governance. The Diligent One Platform gives practitioners, the C-Suite and the board a consolidated view of their entire GRC practice so they can more effectively manage risk, build greater resilience and make better decisions, faster. 

At Diligent, we're building the future with people who think boldly and move fast.  Whether you're designing systems that leverage large language models or part of a team reimaging workflows with AI, you'll help us unlock entirely new ways of working and thinking.  Curiosity is in our DNA, we look for individuals willing to ask the big questions and experiment fearlessly - those who embrace change not as a challenge, but as an opportunity.  The future belongs to those who keep learning, and we are building it together.  At Diligent, you’re not just building the future - you’re an agent of positive change, joining a global community on a mission to make an impact.

Learn more at diligent.com or follow us on LinkedIn and Facebook

What Diligent Offers You 

Creativity is ingrained in our culture. We are innovative collaborators by nature. We thrive in exploring how things can be differently both in our internal processes and to help our clients
We care about our people. Diligent offers a flexible work environment, global days of service, comprehensive health benefits, meeting free days, generous time off policy and wellness programs to name a few
We have teams all over the world. We may be headquartered in New York City, but we have office hubs in Washington D.C., Vancouver, London, Galway, Budapest, Munich, Bengaluru, Singapore, and Sydney.
Diversity is important to us. Growing, maintaining and promoting a diverse team is a top priority for us. We foster and encourage diversity through our Employee Resource Groups and provide access to resources and education to support the education of our team, facilitate dialogue, and foster understanding.

Diligent created the modern governance movement. Our world-changing idea is to empower leaders with the technology, insights and connections they need to drive greater impact and accountability – to lead with purpose. Our employees are passionate, smart, and creative people who not only want to help build the software company of the future, but who want to make the world a more sustainable, equitable and better place. 

Headquartered in New York, Diligent has offices in Washington D.C.,  London, Galway, Budapest, Vancouver, Bengaluru, Munich, Singapore and Sydney.   To foster strong collaboration and connection, this role will follow a hybrid work model. If you are within a commuting distance to one of our Diligent office locations, you will be expected to work onsite at least 50% of the time. We believe that in-person engagement helps drive innovation, teamwork, and a strong sense of community.

We are a drug free workplace. Diligent is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, pregnancy, childbirth, physical disability, mental disability, age, military status, protected veteran status, marital status, registered domestic partner or civil union status, gender (including sex stereotyping and gender identity or expression), medical condition (including, but not limited to, cancer related or HIV/AIDS related), genetic information, or sexual orientation in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Diligent's EEO Policy and Know Your Rights. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, you may contact us at recruitment@diligent.com.

To all recruitment agencies: Diligent does not accept unsolicited agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Diligent employees or any other organization location. Diligent is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Location agnostic
Permanent full time – work with high performing team
Bring clarity to complex problems and leave a measurable mark on the organisation

Make your mark on how performance is recognised and rewarded.

We’re looking for a Performance & Reward Partner to play a key role in shaping how we attract, retain and motivate our people. This role works in a small and highly-skilled P&R team, and is part of a larger People & culture team that directly drives our organisational strategy.

The role

As our Performance & Reward Partner, you will be a subject matter expert to manage the design, development and implementation of remuneration, performance and benefits programs, while playing a central role in driving our annual performance and reward cycles.

Why this role matters

Your work will ensure our people are recognised and rewarded in a way that drives performance, supports our strategy, and ultimately delivers better outcomes for our customers.

What you’ll be doing

Play a pivotal role in shaping a high-performance culture through effective reward strategies
Lead and support the annual performance cycle to drive the right behaviours and outcomes
Drive the annual remuneration review process, ensuring delivery within budget, timelines and compliance requirements
Continuously improve processes to enhance efficiency, simplicity and user experience
Implement organisation-wide total remuneration initiatives aligned to strategic priorities
Partner with leaders and senior stakeholders to provide expert advice on performance, reward and benefits
Support organisational design activities, including role assessment and market benchmarking
Design and deliver education sessions for leaders on performance, remuneration and benefits frameworks
Act as a trusted advisor on both internal and external appointments from a reward perspective

What you’ll bring

Degree qualification (or equivalent experience)
8+ years’ experience in performance, reward or broader HR roles
Strong knowledge of remuneration and benefits, ideally within financial services
Understanding of market trends and benchmarking practices
Experience working with HR systems, particularly performance and reward platforms
Strong analytical capability with the ability to interpret complex data sets
Excellent stakeholder management and consulting skills
Ability to communicate clearly and influence senior stakeholder

To Apply
